menuPopup.menuPopup_1:
You will lose your work if you leave this activity.

Are you sure you want to continue?

p100_s1.p100_s1_2:
Let's investigate trigonometry.

Select an activity below.
p100_s1.p100_s1_3:
Congratulations!

You have completed Trig degrees. Excellent work!
p100_s1.p100_popup_1:
Excellent work!

You have completed
Trig degrees: sine to 360°.

Try investigating a different function.
p100_s1.p100_popup_2:
Excellent work!

You have completed
Trig degrees: cosine to 360°.

Try investigating a different function.
p100_s1.p100_popup_3:
Excellent work!

You have completed
Trig degrees: tangent to 360°.

Try investigating a different function.
p100_s1.p100_s2_6a:
Congratulations!

You have completed Trig degrees.

If you wish to start an activity again, select OK.

Thanks for all your work!
p100_s1.p100_s2_6b: Thanks for your work on finding and graphing cosine!

p101_s1a.p101_s1a_4:
Trigonometry is not just something we have to
learn in class. It is an important tool in geography,
navigation, engineering and other fields.

Trigonometry is extremely useful for solving problems
involving distance and angles. For example, it can be
used to calculate how far a boat is from shore.

p101_s1b.p101_s1b_4:
Trigonometric functions can also model periodic
events, such as the changing depth of water
caused by ocean tides.

This is important information if you are navigating
a boat into a harbour.

p101_s2.p101_s2_1: Let's watch an animation that shows how sine and cosine are defined.

p101_s3.p101_s3_1:
You've just seen how mathematicians use a circle with a radius of 1 unit to define two ratios used in trigonometry: sine and cosine.

Let's review it step by step.
p101_s3.p101_s3_5:
Mathematicians divide up the unit circle into four equal sectors called quadrants. The sectors are always numbered as shown here.

So when someone refers to the third quadrant, everyone knows which sector they are talking about.
p101_s3.p101_s3_8:
To start, we've created an angle.

Let's look at the next step.
p101_s3.p101_s3_12: The circle is called a unit circle because it has a radius of 1 unit.
p101_s3.p101_s3_17:
The length OB is called the cosine of the angle.

In this example, it is the cosine of 35°.
p101_s3.p101_s3_23: The length OA is called the sine of the angle. In this example, it is the sine of 35°. It has the numerical value of 0.5735... (as shown).
p101_s3.p101_s3_23b:
Notice that the lengths PB and OA are the same.

The line PB is often used to define sine in the unit circle because it forms part of the OPB triangle.
p101_s3.p101_s3_25:
Trigonometric ratios can be used to solve measurement problems with right-angled triangles.

Let's see how the unit circle definition of sine relates to right-angled triangles.

p101_s4.p101_s4_7:
The unit circle tool is useful because it allows us to look at the sine of angles greater than 90°.

Let's find out how.
p101_s4.p101_s4_10:
The length OA is the sine of the angle. As OT rotates, the sine of the angle will change.

Use the handle at T to rotate OT around the circle. Watch how the angle and the value of sine changes. You can go all the way around the circle.

p101_s5a.p101_s5a_2:
Your task is to discover the two missing values of sine in the table.

First, locate an angle by moving the handle at T. Then enter its value of sine.

You need to round the values to two decimal places.
p101_s5a.p101_s5a_5:
That's correct!

Now try some questions about the values of sine in the first quadrant.
p101_s5a.p101_s5a_6:
At least one of the sine values you entered is incorrect. Any incorrect values are highlighted in the table.

Fix any incorrect values.
p101_s5a.p101_s5a_7:
At least one of the values is still incorrect.

Remember, if the third decimal place is
5 or more, round the second decimal up.
p101_s5a.p101_s5a_8:
That still wasn't right.

Any values that were incorrect have now been entered for you.

Now try some questions about the values of sine in the first quadrant.

p101_s5b.p101_s5b_2:
In the first quadrant, the range of the sine function is 0 to 1.

If sin Ø = 0.5, what is the angle Ø?
p101_s5b.p101_s5b_7:
That's not right.

Move the handle to find sin Ø = 0.5.
Then check what the angle is.
p101_s5b.p101_s5b_7b:
That's still incorrect.

Remember, if the third decimal place is
5 or more, round the second decimal up.
p101_s5b.p101_s5b_8:
That still wasn't right.

The correct answer has been selected for you.

Now try another question.
p101_s5b.p101_s5b_10:
When the angle moves from the first to the second quadrant, the value of the sine function changes.

What do you think occurs?
p101_s5b.p101_s5b_11a: It continues to increase and remains positive.
p101_s5b.p101_s5b_12:
Excellent!

Let's look at the values of sine in the second quadrant.
p101_s5b.p101_s5b_13:
That's not it.

Move the handle at T and note what happens to the y coordinate of A as it moves into the second quadrant.
p101_s5b.p101_s5b_13b:
That's not it.

Move the handle at T and note what happens to the y coordinate of A as it moves into the second quadrant.
p101_s5b.p101_s5b_14:
That still wasn't right.

The correct answer has been selected for you.

Let's look at the values of sine in the second quadrant.

p101_s6a.p101_s6a_2:
The table now includes angles from the second quadrant.

Move the handle to find 123°, then enter its sine value in the table.

Remember, you need to round the value to two decimal places.
p101_s6a.p101_s6a_5:
That's it!

Now try some questions on the values of sine in the second quadrant.
p101_s6a.p101_s6a_6:
That sine value is incorrect.

Move the handle at T to 123° and check the sine value.
p101_s6a.p101_s6a_7:
That's still incorrect.

Remember, if the third decimal place is
5 or more, round the second decimal up.
p101_s6a.p101_s6a_8:
That still wasn't right.

The correct sine value has been entered for you.

Now try some questions about the values of sine in the second quadrant.

p101_s6b.p101_s6b_2:
In the second quadrant, the sine function appears to be decreasing.

The table below shows that in the first quadrant, sin 41° is 0.66.

In the second quadrant, what angle has a value of sine that is also 0.66?
p101_s6b.p101_s6b_7:
That's not right.

Move the handle to find sin Ø = 0.66 in the second quadrant. Then check what the angle is.
p101_s6b.p101_s6b_7b:
That's not right.

Move the handle to find sin = 0.66 in the second quadrant. Then check what the angle is.
p101_s6b.p101_s6b_8:
That still wasn't right.

The correct answer has been selected for you.

Now try another question.
p101_s6b.p101_s6b_10:
Using the same logic, which angle in the first quadrant has the same sine value as 110°?

Enter your answer below.
p101_s6b.p101_s6b_12:
That's correct!

Choose whether to complete the table of sine values yourself or to have it completed for you.
p101_s6b.p101_s6b_14:
That's still not correct.

Move the handle at T to find the sine value for 110°, then locate the angle with the same sine value in the first quadrant.
p101_s6b.p101_s6b_15:
That still wasn't right.

The correct answer has been entered for you.

Choose whether to complete the table of sine values yourself or to have it completed for you.
p101_s6b.p101_s6b_18:
Move the handle to find the angles and enter the missing sine values in the table.

Remember, you need to round the values to two decimal places.
p101_s6b.p101_s6b_20:
Well done!

Let's look at the values of sine in the third quadrant.
p101_s6b.p101_s6b_21:
At least one of the sine values you entered is incorrect. Any incorrect values are highlighted in the table.

Fix any incorrect values.
p101_s6b.p101_s6b_22:
At least one of the values is still incorrect.

Remember, if the third decimal place is
5 or more, round the second decimal up.
p101_s6b.p101_s6b_23:
That still wasn't right.

Any values that were incorrect have now been entered for you.

Let's look at the values of sine in the third quadrant.

p101_s7a.p101_s7a_2:
The table now includes angles from the third quadrant.

Enter the value of sine for 208° in the table.

Remember, you need to round the value to two decimal places.
p101_s7a.p101_s7a_5:
That's correct!

Now try some questions about the values of sine in the third quadrant.
p101_s7a.p101_s7a_6:
That's incorrect.

Move the handle at T to find the value of sine for 208°.
p101_s7a.p101_s7a_7:
That's still incorrect.

Remember, if the third decimal place is
5 or more, round the second decimal up.
p101_s7a.p101_s7a_8:
That still wasn't right.

The correct value has been entered for you.

Now try some questions about the values of sine in the third quadrant.

p101_s7b.p101_s7b_2:
In the third quadrant, the sine function has become negative.

Which angle in the third quadrant has the lowest value for the sine function?
p101_s7b.p101_s7b_7:
That's not it.

Move the handle at T to find the lowest sine value in the third quadrant.
p101_s7b.p101_s7b_7b:
That's not it.

Move the handle at T to find the lowest sine value in the third quadrant.
p101_s7b.p101_s7b_8:
That still wasn't right.

The correct answer has been selected for you.

Now try another question.
p101_s7b.p101_s7b_10:
What is the minimum value for the sine function in the third quadrant?

Enter the value below.
p101_s7b.p101_s7b_12:
That's correct!

Choose whether to complete the table of sine values yourself or to have it completed for you.
p101_s7b.p101_s7b_14:
That's still not correct.

Move the handle at T to find the lowest value of sine in the third quadrant.
p101_s7b.p101_s7b_15:
That still wasn't right.

The correct answer has been entered for you.

Choose whether to complete the table of sine values yourself or to have it completed for you.
p101_s7b.p101_s7b_18:
Move the handle to find the angles and enter the missing sine values in the table.

Remember, you need to round the values to two decimal places.
p101_s7b.p101_s7b_20:
That's correct!

Let's look at the values of sine in the fourth quadrant.
p101_s7b.p101_s7b_21:
Some of the sine values you entered are incorrect. These are highlighted in the table.

Fix any incorrect values and then select OK.
p101_s7b.p101_s7b_22:
At least one of the values is still incorrect.

Remember, if the third decimal place is
5 or more, round the second decimal up.
p101_s7b.p101_s7b_23:
That still wasn't right.

Any values that were incorrect have now been entered for you.

Let's look at the values of sine in the fourth quadrant.

p101_s8a.p101_s8a_2:
The table now includes angles from the fourth quadrant.

Enter the value of sine for 288°.

Remember, you need to round the value to two decimal places.
p101_s8a.p101_s8a_5:
That's correct!

Now try some questions about the values of sine in the fourth quadrant.
p101_s8a.p101_s8a_6:
That sine value is incorrect.

Move the handle at T to find 288° and check the sine value.
p101_s8a.p101_s8a_7:
That's still incorrect.

Remember, if the third decimal place is
5 or more, round the second decimal up.
p101_s8a.p101_s8a_8:
That still wasn't right.

The correct value has been entered for you.

Now try some questions about the values of sine in the fourth quadrant.

p101_s8b.p101_s8b_2:
You may have noticed that in the fourth quadrant, the sine function is increasing again.

What do you think the value of sine is at the end of the fourth quadrant?

Enter the value below.
p101_s8b.p101_s8b_6:
That's not right.

Move the handle at T to the end of the fourth quadrant and check the value of sine.
p101_s8b.p101_s8b_6b:
That's not right.

Move the handle at T to the end of the fourth quadrant and check the value of sine.
p101_s8b.p101_s8b_7:
That still wasn't right.

The correct answer has been entered for you.

Now try another question.
p101_s8b.p101_s8b_9: What angle in the fourth quadrant has the same value of sine as 251°?
p101_s8b.p101_s8b_13:
That's correct!

Choose whether to complete the table of sine values yourself or to have it completed for you.
p101_s8b.p101_s8b_14:
That's not right.

Move the handle at T to find the sine of 251°, then locate the angle with the same sine value in the fourth quadrant.
p101_s8b.p101_s8b_14b:
That's not right.

Move the handle at T to find the sine of 251°, then locate the angle with the same sine value in the fourth quadrant.
p101_s8b.p101_s8b_15:
That still wasn't right.

The correct answer has been selected for you.

Choose whether to complete the table of sine values yourself or to have it completed for you.
p101_s8b.p101_s8b_18:
Move the handle to find the angles and enter the missing sine values in the table.

Remember, you need to round the values to two decimal places.
p101_s8b.p101_s8b_20:
That's correct!

The sine values for angles in the fourth quadrant increase.
p101_s8b.p101_s8b_21:
At least one of the sine values you entered is incorrect. Any incorrect values are highlighted in the table.

Fix any incorrect values.
p101_s8b.p101_s8b_22:
At least one of the values is still incorrect.

Remember, if the third decimal place is
5 or more, round the second decimal up.
p101_s8b.p101_s8b_23:
That still wasn't right.

Any values that were incorrect have now been entered for you.

p101_s9a.p101_s9a_9: Now that we have the sine values for angles from 0° to 360°, let's see how this looks when plotted on a graph.
p101_s9a.p101_s9a_9b: Move the handle to join the points.
p101_s9a.p101_s9a_13:
This graph helps us understand the properties of sine.

It represents the function:

y = sin x

where every y value on the curve is the sine of the corresponding x value.

p101_s9b.p101_s9b_3: Enter the correct answers in the spaces.
p101_s9b.p101_s9b_6:
Some of the values are incorrect. These are highlighted.

Fix any incorrect values and then select OK.
p101_s9b.p101_s9b_7:
At least one of the values you entered is incorrect. Any incorrect values are highlighted.

Fix any incorrect values.
p101_s9b.p101_s9b_8:
At least one of the values is still incorrect.

Use the tool to find the correct values.
Remember to round the sine values.
p101_s9b.p101_s9b_9:
That's still not right.

Any values that were incorrect have now been entered for you.

Now try another question.
p101_s9b.p101_s9b_12:
Enter the correct answers in the spaces.
Remember to round the sine values to two decimal places.
p101_s9b.p101_s9b_21:
Think of an angle (Ø) in the second quadrant.

Which of the following would be the same as sin (Ø)?
p101_s9b.p101_s9b_26:
That's not correct.

Use the sine graph to help you. Remember that angle (Ø) is in the second quadrant.
p101_s9b.p101_s9b_26b:
That's still not right.

Imagine a horizontal line joining the two angles that have the same value for sine. Work out how the angles are related.
p101_s9b.p101_s9b_29:
The unit circle can be used to find the sine of angles of any size. If we keep moving the point around the unit circle, past the x-axis, we get angles greater than 360°.

Which of the angles below has a sine of –1?
p101_s9b.p101_s9b_33:
That's right!

Now try the final question.
p101_s9b.p101_s9b_34:
That's not correct.

Look at the graph to see where sine has a value of –1.
p101_s9b.p101_s9b_34b:
That's still not correct.

Begin from the x-axis. After doing a full rotation (360°) the point needs to move another 270° to have a sine of –1.
p101_s9b.p101_s9b_35:
That still wasn't right.

The correct answer has been selected for you.

Now try the final question.
p101_s9b.p101_s9b_37: Which of the angles below has the same sine as 165°?
p101_s9b.p101_s9b_41:
That's correct!

The sine value at 165° is the same as the sine value at 15°. The angle 375° is in the same position in the first quadrant as the angle 15° because 375° = 15° + 360°.
p101_s9b.p101_s9b_41b:
That's not correct.

Remember that there is an angle in the first quadrant with the same sine value as an angle in the second quadrant.
p101_s9b.p101_s9b_42:
That's still not right.

Remember that the value of sine at 165° is the same as the sine value at 15°. After doing a complete rotation and then continuing, at which angle would sine have the same value as it does at 15°?
p101_s9b.p101_s9b_43:
That's still not right.

The correct answer has been selected for you.

You've completed all the questions.

p101_s10.p101_s10_3:
We've discovered that the cosine ratio
changes as the size of the angle changes.

The pattern formed by the sine value, in particular,
is easily observed in real life. This wave-shaped
curve is called the sine function. It is used to
describe sound, light, radio and many other
wave forms.

Let's look at some examples.
p101_s10.p101_s10_7:
Congratulations! You have completed Trig degrees.

If you wish to start an activity again, select Menu.

Thanks for all your work!

p102_s3.p102_s3_17: The length OB is called the cosine of the angle. In this example, it is the cosine of 35°. It has the numerical value of 0.8191... (as shown).
p102_s3.p102_s3_23:
The length OA is called the sine of the angle.

In this example, it is the sine of 35°.
p102_s3.p102_s3_25:
Trigonometric ratios can be used to solve measurement problems with right-angled triangles.

Let's see how the unit circle definition of cosine relates to right-angled triangles.

p102_s4.p102_s4_7:
The unit circle tool is useful because it allows us to look at the cosine of angles greater than 90°.

Let's find out how.
p102_s4.p102_s4_10:
The length OB is the cosine of the angle. As OT rotates, the cosine of the angle will change.

Use the handle at T to rotate OT around the circle. Watch how the angle and the value of cosine changes. You can go all the way around the circle.

p102_s5a.p102_s5a_2:
Your task is to discover the two missing values of cosine in the table.

First, locate an angle by moving the handle at T. Then enter its value of cosine.

You need to round the values to two decimal places.
p102_s5a.p102_s5a_5:
That's correct!

Now try a question about the values of cosine in the first quadrant.
p102_s5a.p102_s5a_6:
At least one of the cosine values you entered is incorrect. Any incorrect values are highlighted in the table.

Fix any incorrect values.
p102_s5a.p102_s5a_8:
That still wasn't right.

Any values that were incorrect have now been entered for you.

Now try a question about the value of cosine in the first quadrant.

p102_s5b.p102_s5b_2:
In the first quadrant, the range of the cosine function is 0 to 1. It starts at 1 and decreases to 0.

If cos Ø = 0.5, what is the angle Ø?
p102_s5b.p102_s5b_6:
That's correct!

Now let's look at the values of cosine in the second quadrant.
p102_s5b.p102_s5b_7:
That's not right.

Move the handle to find cos Ø = 0.5.
Then check what the angle is.
p102_s5b.p102_s5b_8:
That still wasn't right.

The correct answer has been selected for you.

Now let's look at the values of cosine in the second quadrant.

p102_s6a.p102_s6a_2:
The table now includes angles from the second quadrant.

Move the handle to find 123°, then enter its cosine value in the table.

Remember, you need to round the value to two decimal places.
p102_s6a.p102_s6a_5:
That's it!

Now try some questions on the values of cosine in the second quadrant.
p102_s6a.p102_s6a_6:
That cosine value is incorrect.

Move the handle at T to 123° and check the cosine value.
p102_s6a.p102_s6a_8:
That still wasn't right.

The correct cosine value has been entered for you.

Now try some questions about the values of cosine in the second quadrant.

p102_s6b.p102_s6b_2:
In the second quadrant, the cosine function appears to be negative.

Which angle has the lowest value of cosine in the second quadrant?
p102_s6b.p102_s6b_7:
That's not right.

Move the handle at T to find the lowest value of cosine in the second quadrant. Then check what the angle is.
p102_s6b.p102_s6b_10:
What is the lowest value of cosine in the second quadrant?

Enter your answer below.
p102_s6b.p102_s6b_12:
That's correct!

Choose whether to complete the table of cosine values yourself or to have it completed for you.
p102_s6b.p102_s6b_14:
That's still not correct.

Move the handle at T to find the lowest value of cosine in the second quadrant.
p102_s6b.p102_s6b_15:
That still wasn't right.

The correct answer has been entered for you.

Choose whether to complete the table of cosine values yourself or to have it completed for you.
p102_s6b.p102_s6b_18:
Move the handle to find the angles and enter the missing cosine values in the table.

Remember, you need to round the values to two decimal places.
p102_s6b.p102_s6b_20:
Well done!

Let's look at the values of cosine in the third quadrant.
p102_s6b.p102_s6b_23:
That still wasn't right.

Any values that were incorrect have now been entered for you.

Let's look at the values of cosine in the third quadrant.

p102_s7a.p102_s7a_2:
The cosine values in the second quadrant continue to decrease. Now the table includes angles from the third quadrant.

Enter the value of cosine for 208° in the table.

Remember, you need to round the value to two decimal places.
p102_s7a.p102_s7a_5:
That's correct!

Now try some questions about the values of cosine in the third quadrant.
p102_s7a.p102_s7a_6:
That's incorrect.

Move the handle at T to find the value of cosine for 208°.
p102_s7a.p102_s7a_8:
That still wasn't right.

The correct cosine value has been entered for you.

Now try some questions about the values of cosine in the third quadrant.

p102_s7b.p102_s7b_2:
In the third quadrant, the cosine function increases.

The table below shows that in the second quadrant, cos 131° is –0.66.

In the third quadrant, what angle has a cosine value that is also –0.66?
p102_s7b.p102_s7b_7:
That's not right.

Move the handle to find cos Ø = –0.66 in the third quadrant. Then check what the angle is.
p102_s7b.p102_s7b_10:
Using the same logic, which angle in the second quadrant has the same cosine as 215°?

Enter your answer below.
p102_s7b.p102_s7b_14:
That's still not correct.

Move the handle at T to find the cosine value for 215°, then locate the angle in the second quadrant with the same cosine value.
p102_s7b.p102_s7b_20:
That's correct!

Let's look at the values of cosine in the fourth quadrant.
p102_s7b.p102_s7b_23:
That still wasn't right.

Any values that were incorrect have now been entered for you.

Let's look at the values of cosine in the fourth quadrant.

p102_s8a.p102_s8a_2:
The table now includes angles from the fourth quadrant.

Enter the value of cosine for 288°.

Remember, you need to round the value to two decimal places.
p102_s8a.p102_s8a_5:
That's correct!

Now try some questions about the values of cosine in the fourth quadrant.
p102_s8a.p102_s8a_6:
That cosine value is incorrect.

Move the handle at T to find 288° and check the cosine value.
p102_s8a.p102_s8a_8:
That still wasn't right.

The correct value has been entered for you.

Now try some questions about the values of cosine in the fourth quadrant.

p102_s8b.p102_s8b_2:
You may have noticed that in the fourth quadrant the cosine function continues to increase.

What do you think the value of cosine is at the end of the fourth quadrant?

Enter the value below.
p102_s8b.p102_s8b_7:
That's still not right.

Move the handle at T to the end of the fourth quadrant and check the value of cosine.
p102_s8b.p102_s8b_7a:
That's still not right.

The correct value has been entered for you.

Now try another question.
p102_s8b.p102_s8b_9: What angle in the fourth quadrant has the same value of cosine as 19°?
p102_s8b.p102_s8b_14:
That's still not right.

Move the handle at T to find the cosine of 19°, then locate the angle with the same cosine value in the fourth quadrant.
p102_s8b.p102_s8b_15:
That's still wasn't right.

The correct answer has been selected for you.

Choose whether to complete the table of cosine values yourself or to have it completed for you.
p102_s8b.p102_s8b_20:
That's correct!

The cosine values for angles in the fourth quadrant increase.
p102_s8b.p102_s8b_21:
At least one of the cosine values you entered is incorrect. Any incorrect values are highlighted in the table.

Fix any incorrect values.
p102_s8b.p102_s8b_23:
That still wasn't right.

Any values that were incorrect have now been entered for you.

p102_s9a.p102_s9a_9: Now that we have the cosine values for angles from 0° to 360°, let's see how this looks when plotted on a graph.
p102_s9a.p102_s9a_9b: Move the handle to join the points.
p102_s9a.p102_s9a_12:
This graph helps us understand the properties of cosine.

It represents the function:

y = cos x

where every y value on the curve is the cosine of the corresponding x value.

p102_s9b.p102_s9b_3:
Enter the correct answers in the spaces.
Remember to round the cosine values to two decimal places.
p102_s9b.p102_s9b_8:
At least one of the values is still incorrect.

Use the tool to find the correct values.
Remember to round the cosine values.
p102_s9b.p102_s9b_21:
Think of an angle (Ø) in the first quadrant.

Which of the following would be the same
as cos (Ø)?
p102_s9b.p102_s9b_26:
That's not correct.

Use the cosine graph to help you. Remember that the angle is in the first quadrant.
p102_s9b.p102_s9b_26b:
That's still not correct.

Imagine a horizontal line joining the two angles that have the same value for cosine. Work out how the angles are related.
p102_s9b.p102_s9b_29:
The unit circle can be used to find the cosine of angles of any size.

Which angle below has a cosine of –1?
p102_s9b.p102_s9b_34:
That's not correct.

Look at the graph to see where cosine has a value of –1.
p102_s9b.p102_s9b_34b:
That's still not correct.

Begin from the x-axis. After doing a full rotation (360°) the point needs to move another 180° to have a cosine of –1.
p102_s9b.p102_s9b_37: Which of the angles below has the same cosine as 65°?
p102_s9b.p102_s9b_41:
That's correct!

The cosine value at 65° is the same as the cosine value at 295°. The angle 655° is in the same position in the fourth quadrant as the angle 295° because 655° = 295° + 360°.
p102_s9b.p102_s9b_41b:
That's not correct.

Remember that there is an angle in the fourth quadrant with the same cosine value as an angle in the first quadrant.
p102_s9b.p102_s9b_42:
That's still not right.

Remember that the value of cosine at 65° is the same as the cosine value at 295°. After doing a complete rotation, and then continuing into the fourth quadrant, at which angle would cosine have the same value as it does at 65°?

p102_s10.p102_s10_6: Thanks for your work on finding and graphing cosine!

p103_s2.p103_s2_1: Let's watch an animation that shows how sine, cosine and tangent are defined.

p103_s3.p103_s3_1:
You've just seen how mathematicians use a circle with a radius of 1 unit to define three ratios used in trigonometry: sine, cosine and tangent.

Let's review it step by step.
p103_s3.p103_s3_24:
The length OA is called the sine of the angle.

In this example, it is the sine of 35°.
p103_s3.p103_s3_29:
The length DQ is called the tangent of the angle. In this example, it is the tangent of 35°. It has the numerical value of 0.7002...
(as shown).
p103_s3.p103_s3_31:
Trigonometric ratios can be used to solve measurement problems with right-angled triangles.

Let's see how the unit circle definition of tangent relates to right-angled triangles.

p103_s4a.p103_s4a_4:
The triangles OPB and OQD are similar.

Select Next to see why.

p103_s4b.p103_s4b_8:
The unit circle tool is useful because it allows us to look at the trigonometry ratios of angles greater than 90°.

Let's find out how.
p103_s4b.p103_s4b_10:
As OT rotates, the values of the tangent, sine and cosine of the angle will change.

Use the handle at T to rotate OT around the circle. Watch how the angle and the values of the trigonometric functions change. You can go all the way around the circle.

p103_s5a.p103_s5a_2:
Your task is to discover the missing value of the tangent in the table below.

First, locate the angle by moving the handle at T. Then enter its tangent value.

You need to round the value to two decimal places.
p103_s5a.p103_s5a_5:
That's correct!

Now try a question about the tangent values in the first quadrant.
p103_s5a.p103_s5a_6:
That tangent value is incorrect.

Move the handle at T until the angle is 76°. Then check the tangent value.
p103_s5a.p103_s5a_8:
That still wasn't right.

The correct tangent value has been entered for you.

Now try a question about the tangent values in the first quadrant.

p103_s5b.p103_s5b_2:
In the first quadrant, the tangent function starts at 0° and gets larger and larger. The tangent at 90° is undefined. Close to 90°, the value of the tangent function becomes infinitely large and positive.

If tan Ø = 1, what is the angle Ø?
p103_s5b.p103_s5b_6:
That's correct!

Let's look at the tangent values in the second quadrant.
p103_s5b.p103_s5b_7:
That's not right.

Move the handle to find tan Ø = 1. Then check what the angle is.
p103_s5b.p103_s5b_8:
That still wasn't right.

The correct answer has been selected for you.

Let's look at the tangent values in the second quadrant.

p103_s6a.p103_s6a_2:
The table now includes angles from the second quadrant.

Move the handle to find 99°, then enter its tangent value in the table.

Remember, you need to round the value to two decimal places.
p103_s6a.p103_s6a_5:
That's it!

Now try some questions on the tangent values in the second quadrant.
p103_s6a.p103_s6a_6:
That tangent value is incorrect.

Move the handle at T to 99° and then check the tangent value.
p103_s6a.p103_s6a_8:
That still wasn't right.

The correct tangent value has been entered for you.

Now try some questions about the tangent values in the second quadrant.

p103_s6b.p103_s6b_2:
In the second quadrant, the tangent function appears to be negative.

Which angle has the largest tangent value in the second quadrant?
p103_s6b.p103_s6b_7:
That's not right.

Move the handle at T to find the largest value of the tangent in the second quadrant. Then check what the angle is.
p103_s6b.p103_s6b_10: What is the maximum value of the tangent in the second quadrant?
p103_s6b.p103_s6b_12:
That's correct!

Choose whether to complete the table of tangent values yourself or to have it completed for you.
p103_s6b.p103_s6b_14:
That's still not correct.

Move the handle at T to find the largest tangent value in the second quadrant.
p103_s6b.p103_s6b_15:
That still wasn't right.

The correct answer has been selected for you.

Choose whether to complete the table of tangent values yourself or have it completed for you.
p103_s6b.p103_s6b_18:
Move the handle to find the angles and enter the missing tangent values in the table.

Remember, you need to round the values to two decimal places.
p103_s6b.p103_s6b_20:
Well done!

Let's look at the tangent values in the third quadrant.
p103_s6b.p103_s6b_21:
At least one of the tangent values you entered is incorrect. Any incorrect values are highlighted in the table.

Fix any incorrect values.
p103_s6b.p103_s6b_23:
That still wasn't right.

Any values that were incorrect have now been entered for you.

Let's look at the tangent values in the third quadrant.

p103_s7a.p103_s7a_2:
Check the values in the table. Do you see a pattern?

Now the table includes angles from the third quadrant. Enter the tangent value for 208° in the table.

Remember, you need to round the value to two decimal places.
p103_s7a.p103_s7a_5:
That's correct!

Now try some questions about the tangent values in the third quadrant.
p103_s7a.p103_s7a_6:
That's incorrect.

Move the handle at T to find the value of the tangent for 208°.
p103_s7a.p103_s7a_8:
That still wasn't right.

The correct tangent value has been entered for you.

Now try some questions about the tangent values in the third quadrant.

p103_s7b.p103_s7b_2:
In the third quadrant, the tangent function is positive and increasing.

The table below shows that in the first quadrant, tan 41° is 0.87.

In the third quadrant, what angle has a tangent value that is also 0.87?
p103_s7b.p103_s7b_7:
That's not right.

Move the handle to find tan Ø = 0.87 in the third quadrant. Then check what the angle is.
p103_s7b.p103_s7b_10:
Using the same logic, which angle in the first quadrant has the same tangent as 262°?

Enter your answer below.
p103_s7b.p103_s7b_14:
That's still not correct.

Move the handle at T to find the tangent value for 262° then locate the angle with the same tangent value in the first quadrant.
p103_s7b.p103_s7b_15:
That still wasn't right.

The correct answer has been entered for you.

Choose whether to complete the table of tangent values yourself or to have it completed for you.
p103_s7b.p103_s7b_20:
That's correct!

Let's look at the tangent values in the fourth quadrant.
p103_s7b.p103_s7b_23:
That still wasn't right.

Any values that were incorrect have now been entered for you.

Let's look at the tangent values in the fourth quadrant.

p103_s8a.p103_s8a_2:
The table now includes angles from the fourth quadrant.

Enter the value of the tangent for 278°.

Remember, you need to round the value to two decimal places.
p103_s8a.p103_s8a_5:
That's correct!

Now try some questions about the tangent values in the fourth quadrant.
p103_s8a.p103_s8a_6:
That tangent value is incorrect.

Move the handle at T to find 278° and check the tangent value.
p103_s8a.p103_s8a_8:
That still wasn't right.

The correct value has been entered for you.

Now try some questions about the tangent values in the fourth quadrant.

p103_s8b.p103_s8b_2:
You may have noticed that in the fourth quadrant the tangent function is negative again.

What do you think the value of the tangent is at the end of the fourth quadrant?

Enter the value below.
p103_s8b.p103_s8b_6:
That's not right.

Move the handle at T to the end of the fourth quadrant and check the value of the tangent.
p103_s8b.p103_s8b_7:
That still wasn't right.

The correct answer has been entered for you.

Now try another question.
p103_s8b.p103_s8b_9: What angle in the fourth quadrant has the same value of the tangent for 119°?
p103_s8b.p103_s8b_14:
That's still not right.

Move the handle at T to find the tangent of 119°, then locate the angle with the same tangent value in the fourth quadrant.
p103_s8b.p103_s8b_20:
That's correct!

The tangent values for angles in the fourth quadrant increase.
p103_s8b.p103_s8b_23:
That still wasn't right.

Any values that were incorrect have now been entered for you.

p103_s9a.p103_s9a_5:
Now that we have the tangent values for
angles from 0° to 360°, let's see how this
looks when plotted on a graph.
p103_s9a.p103_s9a_5a: Let's see what the graph looks like.
p103_s9a.p103_s9a_7:
This graph helps us understand the properties of the tangent.

It represents the function:

y = tan x

where every y value on the curve is the tangent of the corresponding x value.

p103_s9b.p103_s9b_3:
Enter the correct answers in the spaces.
Remember to round the tangent values to two decimal places.
p103_s9b.p103_s9b_8:
At least one of the values is still incorrect.

Look at the pattern of positive and negative values of the tangent in the graph.
p103_s9b.p103_s9b_12: Enter the correct answers in the spaces.
p103_s9b.p103_s9b_17:
At least one of the values is still not right.

Hint: the unit circle showed that tan 45° = 1. Look also at the pattern of positive and negative values of the tangent in the graph.
p103_s9b.p103_s9b_21:
Think of an angle (Ø) in the first quadrant.

Which of the following would be the same as tan (Ø)?
p103_s9b.p103_s9b_26:
That's not correct.

Use the tangent graph to help you. Remember that angle (Ø) is in the first quadrant.
p103_s9b.p103_s9b_26b:
That's still not right.

Imagine a horizontal line joining the two angles that have the same tangent value. Work out how the angles are related.
p103_s9b.p103_s9b_29:
The unit circle can be used to find the tangent of angles of any size.

Which of the angles below has a tangent of –1?
p103_s9b.p103_s9b_34:
That's not correct.

Look at the graph to see where the tangent has a value of –1.
p103_s9b.p103_s9b_34b:
That's still not correct.

The graph shows that the tangent values repeat every 180°, so if the tangent is –1 at 135°, at which angle would the tangent have the same value again?
p103_s9b.p103_s9b_37: Which of the angles below has the same tangent as 65°?
p103_s9b.p103_S9b_41:
That's correct!

The tangent values repeat every 180°.
The angle 425° is in the same position
in the first quadrant as the angle 65°
because 425° = 65° + 360°.
p103_s9b.p103_s9b_42:
That's not right.

Imagine what the graph would look like if it continued.
p103_s9b.p103_s9b_42b:
That's still not right.

Remember that the tangent values repeat every 180°, so if the tangent is 2.14 at 65°, at which of these angles would the tangent have the same value again?

p103_s10.p103_10_6: Thanks for your work on finding and graphing tangent!
